subject: Physics
title: GPS Needs Relativity
description: A micro-lesson that makes GPS Needs Relativity usable.

hook.text: Without Einstein's relativity corrections, your GPS location would drift by 10 kilometers per day.

buildup.text: GPS satellites orbit at high speed, which slows their clocks. But weaker gravity up there speeds them.

discovery.text: The two effects don't cancel. Satellite clocks gain about 38 microseconds per day over ground clocks.

twist.text: 38 microseconds sounds tiny, but light travels 11 kilometers in that time. Errors compound fast.

climax.text: Every GPS satellite adjusts its clock using both special and general relativity. Einstein is in your pocket.

punchline.text: Your phone finds you because Einstein was right.

quiz.question: Why would GPS be inaccurate without relativity corrections?
options: Satellite clocks tick differently due to speed and gravity
Satellites orbit too fast to send signals
Radio waves bend in the atmosphere
